title: Memory Forensics Playbook

headings.text: Preparing the image: profile & basic checks
Processes: what to collect and why
File and filesystem traces in RAM
Small cheat sheet: commands & quick examples

overview: Memory forensics is the process of analyzing a RAM image to reconstruct the system's runtime state at the time of capture. It surfaces ephemeral evidence — running processes, loaded modules, network sockets, decrypted content, and in-memory malware — that often does not persist to disk.

study.overview: Memory forensics is the process of analyzing a RAM image to reconstruct the system's runtime state at the time of capture. It surfaces ephemeral evidence — running processes, loaded modules, network sockets, decrypted content, and in-memory malware — that often does not persist to disk.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: Preparing the image: profile & basic checks
Processes: what to collect and why
File and filesystem traces in RAM
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

importantCommands.code:
# image identification
vol.py -f memory.img imageinfo

# profile-finding
vol.py -f memory.img kdbgscan --output=kdbg.txt

# process listing
vol.py -f memory.img --profile=Win10x64_#### pslist

# network scan
vol.py -f memory.img --profile=Win10x64_#### netscan

# find suspicious executable pages
vol.py -f memory.img --profile=Win10x64_#### malfind

# print registry key from memory
vol.py -f memory.img --profile=Win10x64_#### printkey -K "\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run"
